Patch Set 9: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 9:

Since it needs refreshing could this patch be based on top of LU-6137 to avoid a merge conflict.

Patch Set 9:

Actually it is the next patch in this series that conflicts.

Patch Set 9:

James, while this short stack of mods could be rebased on the LU-6137 fix as you suggest I would prefer to keep them independent in the interest of not forcing landings in any particular order.  If LU-6137 lands first this can be rebased then.  If this series lands first LU-6137 can be rebased then.

Keeping them separate allows us to avoid multiple retests of good changes, each of which opens us up to hitting unrelated test problems & blocking landings.

Patch Set 9:

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.6/x86_64: (https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sessions/224485c2-c10e-11e4-9d86-5254006e85c2).  Ran 6 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 9: Code-Review-1

ldiskfs/Makefile.in handles fiemap.h since it is a extra added header file. With it gone you get this error.

Replacing 'ext4' with 'ldiskfs': acl.h ext4.h ext4_extents.h ext4_jbd2.h mballoc.h xattr.h acl.c balloc.c bitmap.c block_validity
.c dir.c ext4_jbd2.c extents.c file.c fsync.c hash.c ialloc.c inode.c ioctl.c mballoc.c migrate.c move_extent.c namei.c resize.c 
super.c symlink.c xattr.c xattr_security.c xattr_trusted.c xattr_user.c fiemap.hsed: can't read linux-stage/fs/ext4/fiemap.h: No 
such file or directory

Plus I see left overs from the ext3 days in that Makefile.in. That could be cleaned up.

Patch Set 9:

I did notice that build error about fiemap.h but didn't comment on it as it doesn't stop successful build and is only seen in some environments, for example el7.  since it didn't cause any harm & seems to still be needed in older environments I didn't -review about it.  Looked like more trouble than it's worth to clean it up.  just an opinion

Patch Set 9:

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.6/x86_64: (https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sessions/3b6e8b0e-c13d-11e4-9d86-5254006e85c2).  Ran 13 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 9:

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.6/x86_64: (https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sessions/5903a38e-c142-11e4-b948-5254006e85c2).  Ran 7 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 9: Verified+1

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.6/x86_64: (https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sessions/18cb94e4-c15a-11e4-bef2-5254006e85c2).  Ran 18 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 9:

Bob, doesn't this patch get rid of all the old fiemap patches?  In that case we don't want to leave old cruft behind, since that would just need another patch to clean up later.

Patch Set 9: Code-Review-1

Andreas, I think you are correct.  I now believe I was looking at and comparing an el7 build with this patch against an el6 build without it.  I thought I was looking at both builds with the patch.  I now think the build error should be cleaned up even though it's not a fatal showstopper.

Patch Set 9:

James, the fix for LU-6137 has landed so I expect that will be a non-issue after the LU-6030 mods are rebased.

Patch Set 9:

Also we can use this refresh to cleanup the ldiskfs/Makefile.in of the ext3 craft and fiemap.h handling.
Uploaded patch set 10.

parents.subject: LU-6215 osc: use list_for_each_entry_safe() when delete items

commit.subject: LU-6030 ldiskfs: clean up ext4-fiemap patch
commit.message:
LU-6030 ldiskfs: clean up ext4-fiemap patch

Move ext4-fiemap patch to osd-ldiskfs. So we can
remove this patch entirely.

Signed-off-by: Yang Sheng <yang.sheng@intel.com>
Change-Id: I639733f6f106398bbc3d5e2ffc6fa8a06ffe867f
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/13571
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Bob Glossman <bob.glossman@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
